case.question: What is SENS motion®?
case.gold_answer: SENS motion is an integrated system for collecting physical activity data from groups of people.

ActivityMonitoringApp.description: A software application designed to facilitate the collection and management of activity data for research purposes.

HealthIndicator.description: A measurable value that can be used to assess the health of an individual or population, often used in research studies to predict or monitor health outcomes.

HealthcareSensor.description: A device or system designed to monitor and record physical activity or vital signs for healthcare purposes.

PhysicalObject.description: Any Object that has a proper space region. Examples include people, equipment, sensors, and buildings.

SensorPatch.description: A wearable device, often a small piece of clothing or adhesive, that is used to monitor various health-related metrics.

SocialObject.description: Any Object that exists only within some social context, such as organizations, laws, plans, or projects.

case.question: What is SENS motion® – For Healthcare?
case.gold_answer: SENS motion® – For Healthcare is a system that enables the use of the SENS motion® activity sensor in healthcare environments such as hospitals or care centres. (source: _index.en::c0000)

case.question: What is SENS motion® – For Research?

outgoing_data_properties.value: Patients will be provided with a tablet in the room where the motivation app is installed. The app will motivate patients to better self-mobilize by giving them a guided virtual tour of Copenhagen. On the app, patients can follow their own activity overview, see how far in the tour they have come, the number of steps and how much active time they have had, both that day and in the last week
It is recommended to read the user manual before using SENS motion

The motivation app will motivate patients to better self-mobilize by giving them a guided virtual tour of Copenhagen. On the app, patients can follow their own activity overview, see how far in the tour they have come, the number of steps and how much active time they have had, both that day and in the last week

The motivation app will motivate patients to better self-mobilize by giving them a guided virtual tour of Copenhagen. On the app, patients can follow their own activity overview, see how far in the tour they have come, the number of steps and how much active time they have had, both that day and in the last week

Staff will have access to an overview screen, where they can easily get an overview of patients' physical activity, including when patients were last mobilized. This will allow staff to prioritise which patients are most important to help with mobilization
